items.prompt: interprets scatter plots and uses informal linear-fit reasoning to describe bounded bivariate data
items.expected_evidence: draw or evaluate an informal line of best fit for roughly linear data
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner interprets the scatter structure and uses a reasonable informal fit aligned to the data pattern.

items.prompt: explains what direction, strength, and form mean in a scatter plot and why association alone does not prove causation
items.expected_evidence: justify what association claims are supported and what causal claims are not supported by the data alone
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains association and fit meaning without overclaiming causal interpretation.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to outlier_trend_erasure.
items.expected_evidence: describe direction, form, strength, and unusual features of bounded scatter plots
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies how an outlier or cluster changed the association claim or fit judgment.

items.prompt: interprets bivariate data in a less-familiar context and checks whether a linear trend claim or prediction is actually supported
items.expected_evidence: estimate or compare predicted versus observed values in bounded contexts
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves fit meaning and limits of prediction in a varied context.
